# R2.02 · Distributed locking

> Mutual exclusion across processes. The generic primitive is one Redis command — SET resource:lock <token> NX PX —
> NX admits one holder, PX makes the lock mortal, the token makes release safe (the contrast). Grounded in EchoMQ's
> real applied form: the claim lease — `@claim` does `ZADD active (now + lease_ms)` on the server clock
> (`redis.call('TIME')`), leasing a job to one worker, with `attempts` (`HINCRBY`) as the fencing token; `@complete`
> and `@retry` refuse a stale token (`EMQSTALE … token mismatch`), and `EchoMQ.Consumer` runs `Jobs.reap` on a cadence
> to return an expired lease to pending. Served at /redis-patterns/coordination/distributed-locking.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/coordination/distributed-locking): the lock as a claim lease, the fencing token, and its limits.
- [SET NX PX](/redis-patterns/coordination/distributed-locking/set-nx-px): the generic atomic acquire — NX for one holder, PX against deadlock, a token for safe release; why SETNX + EXPIRE is a race. The applied form is the lease-scored `active` set.
- [Fencing tokens](/redis-patterns/coordination/distributed-locking/fencing-tokens): the real one — `attempts` (monotone via `HINCRBY`) is the fence; a worker whose lease was reaped and re-claimed carries a stale token and is refused at complete (`EMQSTALE`).
- [Lease renewal](/redis-patterns/coordination/distributed-locking/lease-renewal): the generic one-timer renewal; EchoMQ's applied form is the consumer holding the lease for `:lease_ms` (default 30_000) and `Jobs.reap` recovering an expired one on the server clock.

## Up

- [R2 · Coordination](/redis-patterns/coordination) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
